summary: Typosquat republish of @babylonjs/smart-filters forcing install of @onerjs/core sibling
details: This package is a renamespaced copy of Babylon.js's @babylonjs/smart-filters, republished under the unrelated @onerjs scope. The package.json description still reads 'Babylon.js Smart Filter core' and the repository URL points at BabylonJS/Babylon.js, but every import has been rewritten from @babylonjs/* to @onerjs/*. Critically, the package declares a peerDependency on @onerjs/core (^7.47.3 || ^8.0.1) — a sibling typosquat of @babylonjs/core that does not correspond to any legitimate publisher. Developers who mistakenly install @onerjs/smart-filters (or who are misled by its Babylon.js branding) are forced to also install @onerjs/core from the same unknown publisher, pulling attacker-controlled code into the dependency tree. The smart-filters tarball itself contains no lifecycle scripts or network calls; the harm is delivered through the forced sibling dependency, which is the carrier in this two-package namespace-abuse pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
